title: The Meaning Of The Massacre of Maravilla Mountain
description: The massacre at Maravilla Mountain refers to two young Puerto Rican pro-independence activists who were murdered in a Puerto Rico Police (supported by the US forces) ambush. Juan Antonio Corretjer gives the perspective and analysis of the Puerto Rican Socialist League on the murders and the struggle for Puerto Rican Independence.

title: Discurso Pronunciado por Juan Antonio Corretjer
description: Discurso Pronunciado por Juan Antonio Corretjer, Secretaio-General, Liga Socialista Puertorriquena, En Nueva York, Junio 2, 1978.

title: La Enfermedad Senil del Derechismo en El Comunismo
description:
I. Movimiento Obrero, Paralisis Sindical
II. Movimiento Estudiantil
III. Desarrollo de la Conciencia
publisher: Los Cuadros Estudiantiles de la Liga Socialista Puertorriquena

title: The Second International Conference in Solidarity with Puerto Rican Independence
description: Statement regarding the decision of the Puerto Rican Socialist League to not attend the Second International Conference of Solidarity with the Independence of Puerto Rico. They state that because the objective of the conference is to back to the resolution of the UN Committee on Decolonization which offers the alternative of "free association" to that of independence they cannot attend.
publisher: Central Committee of The Puerto Rican Socialist League

title: Discussion with Don Juan Antonio Corretjer on Puerto Rico and World Situation
description: A discussion with Juan Antonio Correjter about how the Puerto Rican struggle fits into the larger world situation and global politics.

title: El Socialista Numero Especial
description: Issue dedicated to a short biography of Juan Antonio Corretjer.

title: La LSP Ante La ONU 1982
description: Contenido: Ponencia Ante La ONU: Leida por nuestro Sec. Gen. Juan Antonio Corretjer el 2 de agosto de 1982 ante el Comite de Descolonizacion. Duelo Y Rectificacion: Por Juan Antonio Corretjer Reproducido de el Nuevo Dia 27 de julio de 1982 Futuro Sin Falla: Prologo a la segunda edicion proxima a salir en Chicago.

title: La Guerrilla Urbana
description:
Prologo por Juan Antonio Corretjer
I. De la guerra de guerrillas a la guerra de movimientos
II. La Estrategia de la guerrilla
III. Las tacticas de la guerrilla; las tacticas que usa la guerrilla urbana
IV. La organizacion de la guerrilla
V. El metodo de la guerrilla
VI. Revaloracion de la guerrilla Tupamara
VII. Examen Critico de Revolucion en la Revolucion de Regis DeBray

title: History of the Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
description: Includes: History of Control Unit Prisons; How we Define "Control Unit"; History of the Campaign; Structure. Part of 6/16/1995 mailing.
publisher: Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: Statement of Bonnie Kerness on the HISTORY OF CONTROL UNITS
description: Statement on the proliferation and use of Control unit Prisons. Call to action.
publisher: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: Action Proposal from the Committee to End the Mation Lockdown
description: Regarding action component of the national campaign.
publisher: Committee to End the Marion Lockdown
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: Join the Control Unit Emergency Response Network
description: The Control Unit Emergency Response Network consists of a group of people willing to respond to emergency situations inside of control unit prisons by phoning or faxing prison administrators, political figures or media depending on the circumstances.
publisher: Committee to End the Marion Lockdown
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: Abolish!
description: A Call to Action; No Exit Criteria: Northeast Hearings on control unit prisons; Midwest Regional Hearing: Control Units, Prisons and Political Prisoners; Seeking Justice for the Imprisoned: West Coast builds anti-control unit work; Colorado's People's Hearing: The People speak out on the racist imprisonment binge, control units and political prisoners.
publisher: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: Report on the Emergency Response Activated Around Marion by the National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
description: A report about the successes and shortcomings of the activation of the emergency response network around Marion Prison.
publisher: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons [brochure]
description: brochure on history of control units and the campaign. Part of 11/1995 mailing.
publisher: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: The Use of Control Unit Prisons in the United States: Interim Report
description: Information on the proliferation of control unit prisons in the US prison system. Includes: General information; Conditions of Confinement; Placement and Exit criteria for all control unit type facilities.
publisher: The Monitoring Project of the National Campaign to Stop control unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons How To Packet
description: Includes forms for gathering information on nearby control units, conditions of living and incarceration; Copy of Essay "From Alcatraz to Marion to ADX Florence: The Nightmare Escalates" by Jan Susler; Weinstein, Dr. Corey M.D. "Fighting the American Gulag." Third Force v.2 n.6 Jan/Feb. 1995 pp. 24-27.
publisher: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons
collection_name: National Campaign to Stop Control Unit Prisons

title: Eldridge Cleaver Presidency Campaign Press Release
description: Press release/statement re: why Cleaver should be elected President.
publisher: Eldridge Cleaver for President Committee, Peace and Freedom Party, BPP

title: An Undying Love Flyer
description: Flyer: "release Eldridge Cleaver rally," at Parole Office in San Francisco, with Stokely Carmichael, Bobby Seale, and Kathleen Cleaver.

title: Elsa Knight Thompson Letter in response to Cyrill Abels
description: Letter in response to Cyrill Abels re: Eldridge Cleaver having rights to talk he gave on KPFA re: Bobby Hutton shooting, and correspondence leading up to this KPFA clarification letter.

title: Release Eldridge Cleaver: An appeal for letters demanding justice from the California Adult Authority
description: Appeal for letters to get political prisoner Eldridge Cleaver out of Vacaville, California.

title: Plea for Support in the Release of Eldridge Cleaver
description: Letter to Elsa Knight Thompson asking for her support in effort to release Eldridge Cleaver; organization's "Bay Area Co-Chairmen" include James Baldwin, Ossie Davis, Dr. Carlton Goodlett.
publisher: International Committee to Release Eldridge Cleaver

title: Jaco-bin: Eldridge Cleaver for President
description: A publication of the Peace and Freedom Party. Articles includes "Cleaver enters Pres. Race"; "Berkeley begs bread to stop Panthers, PFM"; "A PFM Exclusive! Excerpts from official Berkeley report to US government (re: BPP)"; "write in Dick Gregory for president"

title: Eldridge Cleaver Decision
description: Solano Superior Court Judge Raymond Sherwin ruled that Cleaver's parole was improperly suspended after April shootout (in which Bobby Hutton killed) and reinstated Cleaver's parole.

title: Eldridge Cleaver Talks
description: Press release re: Cleaver accusing Oakland Police of murdering Bobby Hutton on April 6, 1968

title: Chant for Our Times
description: Poem/spoken word from Eldridge Cleaver.

title: Eldridge Cleaver From Algiers
description: Position on drug culture in U.S. as it relates to carrying out the revolutionary struggle

title: International Committee to Release Eldridge Cleaver
description: Lengthy letter requesting support for release from imprisonment of Eldridge Cleaver.
publisher: International Committee to Release Eldridge Cleaver

title: Eldridge Cleaver - US Obstacle - [mp3]
description: Eldridge Cleaver speaks at a noon rally at UC Berkeley about U.S. oppression and imperialism.

title: Eldridge Cleaver - Tactics [mp3]
description: Eldridge Cleaver speaks at a noon rally at UC Berkeley about resistance tactics.

title: Stamp Out Racist Graffiti June 21, 1986
description: Sticker promoting the Stamp Out Racist Graffiti event on June 21.

title: Death to the Klan! Special Edition Winter 1981
description: Newspaper of the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee. In this special edition issue: Revolutionary Struggle Escalates as Government Wages Terrorist Assault; Black Liberation Army Communique on the strategic alliance of the armed military forces of the Revolutionary Nationalist and Anti-Imperialist Movement; Memorial Service Honoring New Afrikan Freedom Fighter Mtayari Shabaka Sundiata; Statement by the provisional government of the Republic of New Afrika regarding the arrest of Fulani Sunni Ali; Chai

title: On Lesbian/Gay Day Quarantine the KKK/Nazis
description: Flyer promoting a march on Lesbian/Gay Pride Day to speak out against increasing right-wing attacks, acts of racism, anti-woman, and anti-gay attacks in Chicago.
publisher: Dykes and Gay Women Against Racism

title: Letter: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan to John Brown Anti-Klan Committee
description: A letter from the KKK to the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee in Chicago. The KKK presents their side of the story regarding a violent altercation at a gathering in Chicago and places blame on the JBAKC for the altercation stating that they are the ones filled with hate. They warn the JBAKC that their movement is growing in the Chicago area and they are planning a summer campaign that will make Chicago known as the racist capital of the world.

title: Build A Movement Against the Klan
description: Flyer promoting the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee's First National Conference on July 4, 1982 in Chicago. This National Conference was called to build a broader and stronger movement against the Klan and white supremacy.

title: They Must Be Stopped! The Growing Klan Threat
description: Flyer promoting a forum with speakers Akinyele Umoja, Stan McKinney, Rev. Lorenzo Tucker, and Shirley Waheed discussing the Klan/Nazi activities in their communities.

title: White, Right, and Looking for a Fight
description: The Chicago Reader's cover story "White, Right, and Looking for a Fight: Has Chicago been targeted by a new alliance of white supremacists?" examines the recent confrontations between the antiracist marchers of the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee and a coalition of Ku Klux Klan and neo-Nazis at the "Stamp Out Racist Graffiti" event on June 21, 1986. It also takes a close look at who these emerging white supremacist groups are, the effects of the racially changing neighborhoods, and the KKK and ne

title: Stop the Klan!
description: Flyer promoting a forum to discuss how to build a movement against the Klan who has committed acts of violence and hatred towards black people in major cities like Los Angeles, Miami, and Chicago in recent years. Akinyele Umoja speaks on the current anti-klan struggle and Los Angeles.

title: Stop Police Terror!
description: Flyer promoting a demonstration at the Belmont police station in Chicago to stop police terror, defend the human rights of Black and Third World people, and to remember Fred Hampton who gave his life for the liberation of Black people.

title: No Imperialist War, No KKK, No Fascist USA!
description: Flyer promoting forum with speaker Chokwe Lumumba to discuss the, the U.S. invading sovereign nations abroad, the KKK's paramilitary training, killer cops, political repression, and the war against Black people.

title: Take a Stand Against the Klan! October 1983
description: Reports on recent incidences of racist attacks against Black people in which the justice system has failed to put away the white attackers. These stories were not carried by most Chicago news outlets, leaving white people with a distorted view of the presence of racism and violence against Black and Third World people around them. Includes a map of the Chicagoland White Supremacist attacks.

title: Oppose The Klan and Nazis on Gay Pride Day!
description: Flyer promoting a counter-rally to oppose the KKK and take a stand against all acts of racist, anti-woman and anti-gay violence in Lincoln Park in Sunday, June 29 1986.

title: Stop Nazi Rally!
description: Flyer calling for unity to stop the Klan and Nazi groups who are holding a rally in Lincoln Park.

title: Say No To Racist Violence
description: An open letter to the people of Chicago concerning the escalation of racist activity by the white supremacist groups in Chicago published in the Chicago Reader. It also promotes the Stamp Out Racist Graffiti event on Saturday, June 21 and the Oppose the Klan and Nazis on Gay Pride Day event on Sunday, June 29.

title: Oppose Racist Violence!
description: An update on the Klan and Nazi racist activity, attacks, and murders against in and around Chicago. It's stated that the way to stop the Klan and Nazis is not to ignore them but to show the Klan that they, JBAKC members and those who oppose racism in any form, they will not tolerate their presence in their neighborhoods and schools and will not tolerate the violation of black people's human rights. There's a call to show support for the black citizens in Zion by writing a letter to the mayor sta

title: Take a Stand Against The Klan and Nazis
description: A flyer promoting a demonstration in Grant Park on June 27 for all progressive people to take a stand against the mobilization of the Nazis, Klan, and Moral Majority. Participants are urged to join the rally and express their solidarity with the national liberation struggles that are leading the the struggle against fascism and repression.

title: Protesters Fight Paint With Paint
description: Article in the Chicago Tribune covering the Stamp Out Racist Graffiti protest on June 21.

title: Oppose the Uptown Cross-Burning!
description: Flyer promoting a demonstration and rally in response to a cross-burning in an integrated area of the Uptown neighborhood in Chicago.

title: We Don't Want Naziism in Our Neighborhood!
description: Flyer promoting a community meeting to discuss ways people can work together to rid the Lakeview neighborhood of racism. This is in response to a group of neo-nazis spreading racist flyers around the neighborhood.

title: Had Enough Whitey?
description: Flyer promoting a counter rally in Marquette Park at the same time and place as a peace and unity rally being held by members of the Martin Luther King Jr. Movement.
publisher: Knights of the Ku Klux Klan

title: Letter: Chicago Police Department to Stanley Weinberger
description: Letter from Joseph DiLeonardi, the Commander of the 19th District Police Station in Chicago, to Stanley Weinberger, the Vice-President of The American Jewish Committee's Chicago Chapter, explaining the police's involvement in at the Stamp Out Racist Graffiti rally and an altercation between members of the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee and the Neo-Nazi group on September 28, 1985.

title: Racist Gangs in Chicago Are Growing More Arrogant
description: Flyer promoting protests against the KKK's "white pride" rally on June 28 and "anti-queer" rally on June 29. Calling for workers, Black people, immigrants, and all anti-racists to stand up in militant protest.

title: Letter: Alan Berkman to John Brown Anti-Klan Committee
description: A letter from Alan Berkman to the JBAKC explaining why he will not be joining in the Coalition action in Uptown. He states that his problem with the JBAKC is in their inability to be consistent with their politics. He doesn't believe the coalition, which is predominantly white, is doing enough and consulting with the people before organizing in their communities.

title: Letter: Laura Whitehorn and Alan Berkman to John Brown Anti-Klan Committee
description: In a letter to the John Brown Anti-Klan Committee, Laura Whitehorn constructively critiques a consistent political problem present in their Death to the Klan! newspaper. Whitehorn states that DTTK! fails to support the struggle of Black people for self-determination- the right of New Afrikan/Afro-American people to land and independence. And because of this failure, she thinks the papers also suffers from a weakness in failing to wage a direct struggle against white supremacy.

title: The Outlaw July-August 1972
description: Cover Story: Unfinished Business: The Last Unorganized Laborers in America.

title: The Outlaw [Vol. 1-1, Nov 1971]
description: First issue of the Outlaw, Journal of the Prisoners Union. Includes a section on Students for Prison Change out of SFSU; Poetry; information about the Prisoners' Union Guerrilla Theater Company, and more.

title: The Outlaw [Vol. 1-2]
description: Editorial on Prison Reform. Articles on the significance of unity, facts about women prisoners, poetry, the case of Joseph Romero, and more.

title: The Outlaw [Vol. 1-3]
description: In this issue, Prisoners' Union pickets AA and DMV; women and men unite; Adult Authority meets to decide on a new contingency parole plan; and more.

title: Peoples Witness - September 26, 1974
description: Cover story is "Courthouse and CDC Demonstrations". Also includes "Resolution of Internal Struggle" explaining why this will be the last issue of the publication; "Inez Garcia & Fred Madrano: People's Right of Self Defense"; "Martin Sostre Communique"; "Unionism vs. Internationalism"; and more. Includes photographs and illustrations, as well as a poem by Beulah Richardson called "A Black Woman Speaks on White Womanhood".

title: Peoples Witness - December 15, 1973
description: Cover story is "Lock-Up! - An Analysis", followed by statements from people in San Quentin, Folsom, CMF Vacaville, and Soledad Prisons. Also includes a poem by Darryl Wilson called "The Divided States of America".

title: Peoples Witness - November 15, 1975
description: Cover story is "Folsom 'Hole' on Strike!" outlining the details of the strike including reasons for striking, how prisoners intend to protest, and information about conditions in the prison. Also includes a poem by Jomo Majizil called "Essence of Man".

title: Peoples Witness - February 15, 1974
description: Cover story is "On the Need for General Demands" about the need to unify the prison movement behind a list of general demands. Also includes information about the Graham-Allen Case involving two men at Tracy charged with the death of a prison guard, a position statement on the Symbionese Liberation Army, and discussion of the meaning of Lumpen Proletariat.

title: Peoples Witness - April 15, 1974
description: Cover story is "Self Criticism on the 'People's Lawsuit'" addressing the issue of white organizations such as the Sacramento Solidarity Committee hesitating to criticize people of color out of fear of causing offense. Other stories include an update on the development of a list of General Demands from the prison movement, a discussion of international political prisoners, and ideas about class structure in the United States.

title: Peoples Witness - May 15, 1974
description: Cover story is "'Rebellion' in Folsom Hole" focusing on recent news coverage of incidents in California Prisons. Also includes a discussion of the Aryan Brotherhood prison gang, an article about the American Nazi Movement, and an open letter to the Oakland Based Black Panther Party in response to an editorial about Huey P. Newton being targeted by the Symbionese Liberation Army, as well as a poem titled "Alone Like a Dog in the Street".

title: Peoples Witness - August 15, 1974
description: Cover story is "Further Internal Struggles of the SSC" discussing the Sacramento Solidarity Committee's work to define itself. Also includes a communique from the Weather Underground in support of the Symbionese Liberation Army, discussion of the role the murder of George Jackson and the Attica Massacre played in the revolutionary struggle, a story about the torture of women who are political prisoners in Chile, analysis of the Aryan Brotherhood, discussion of Ronald Reagan pleading on behalf of

title: Peoples Witness - March 15, 1974
description: Cover story is a discussion of a communique about the "Peoples Lawsuit" against Huey P. Newton and the Oakland Based Black Panther Party. Also includes ideas about the Lumpen Proletariat, and the concept of "conscious and unconscious political prisoners" as including people imprisoned for economic crimes.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [June 1967]
description: In this issue, Tom Hayden reports on the current situation in Puerto Rico after visiting by invitation of the Movimiento Pro-Independencia (MPI); coverage of the fight for the liberation of South Africa, with an emphasis on exploitative labor practices; a US-backed military junta takes power in Greece, adopting the slogan "Stop Communism"; and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [July 1967]
description: In this issue, William Pomeroy reports on the current status of Philippine Political Prisoners; in Haiti, collusion between the Haitian dictator Duvalier and Dominican President Joaquin Balaguer results in the prosecution of Haitian political refugees; 106 clerical workers continue a several months-long strike at the Calcutta, India office of Caltex Oil Company; and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [August 1967]
description: In this issue, President of the Movemiento Pro-Independencia (MPI) Youth in Puerto Rico, Juan Angel Silen, delivers a teach-in on the situation in Puerto Rico in New York City; a report on Israeli aggression and oil export at the Haifa refinery; brief reports on the current situation of U.S. imperialism in different parts of the globe, including Thailand, Vietnam, South Korea, and the Philippines; and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [November 1967]
description: In this issue, excerpts from Fidel Castro's speech to the first conference of the Organization of Latin American Solidarity (OLAS); Frantz Fanon on the assassination of Patrice Lumumba and the Congo's capitulation to the United Nations; 36 South West Africans accused of inciting revolution are tried in the Supreme Court in Pretoria; and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [December 1967]
description: This issue republishes a statement smuggled out of Regis Debray's prison cell on clandestinity and publicity, commitment to revolutionary movement, and his experience of interrogation by the CIA. It also includes a report on Guerrilla War in the Portuguese colonial territory of Guinea-Bissau and an article which alleges that the CIA had been altering the field dairy of Che Guevara. Additional Contents: The Situation in Peru; Uraguay: Model State in a Pickle; Doldrums in the Philippines; and Repo

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [January 1968]
description: In this issue, excerpts from an interview with FRELIMO president, Eduardo Modlane, describe the merger of three parties under a unified revolutionary movement, major policy lines, and military strategy against the Portuguese Army; a report on increases in U.S. aid to centers of higher learning in Latin America; a letter from Phan Thi Quyen, widow of South Vietnamese hero Nguyen Van Troi who attempted to assassinate Defense Secretary McNamara in 1964, addresses the U.S. youth who demonstrated dur

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [Feb 1968]
description: In this issue, an article covers U.S. attempts to expand the Panama Canal despite protest among Panama's left-nationalist movement and describes U.S. maneuvers to undermine Panamanian sovereignty; anti-communist violence in Guatemala escalates; recent victories of the National Liberation Front in South Vietnam; and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [March 1968]
description: This issue examines US aid to anti-revolutionary efforts in Guatemala and includes an article by Jean-Paul Sarte on South Africa and apartheid. It also features a report by Agostino Neto, President of the Angola Popular Liberation Movement, on the current situation of Angola's fight for liberation, and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [April 1968]
description: This issue examines competing political forces in Eritrea throughout the country's history and the emergence of the Liberation Front of Eritrea as a leading revolutionary force. It also considers the relationship between the U.S. and Paraguay; the escalating situation in Cyprus; the declaration of armed struggle in Haiti; and more.

title: Tri-continental Information Center Bulletin [May 1968]
description: This issue includes an interview with Mr. Sharfudine Kahn, representative of FRELIMO, on the conditions of the masses in Mozambique, the history of the resistance movement in the country, and the aims and general strategy of FRELIMO. It also contains information about the struggle for independence in Rhodesia and the Dominican Republic.

title: Finding Aid
description: Finding Aid

title: Abolish Rockefeller's Prison Committee: Immediate Implementation of the Prisoner's Demands
description: This flyer calls for Rockefeller's Prison Committee to be abolished, providing context for some of the crimes related to Attica which took place under Rockefeller, and for the immediate implementation of prisoner's demands.

title: Alderson Attica Demands
description: This pamphlet includes a list of twenty-eight demands issued by prisoners at Attica State Prison in New York in May, 1971, and 42 demands issued by women prisoners at Alderson Federal Prison, which were presented during a four day sit-in in solidarity with Attica prisoners during the week of September 14, 1971. Many of these demands refer to basic rights such as access to adequate food, water, and shelter. Other demands relate to parole, work programs, censorship, and more. The pamphlet also fea

title: Alternative Legal Systems -- China
description: This article provides analysis of how the prison system, judicial system, and law enforcement operate in socialist China, in order to better understand potential alternatives to the current criminal justice system in the US.

title: December 20 -- Our Theory Will Unite With Our Practice... Afeni Shakur
description: This statement emphasizes how economic hardship and the lack of basic necessities such as food and adequate shelter drive women to commit small crimes which lead to their imprisonment. It explores the inhumane treatment of women throughout legal proceedings and jail time when being charged for crimes, and provides information about efforts among an emerging group of women in New York committed to ensuring communication between women inside and outside to better understand the needs inside, offer
publisher: New York 21 Communications Black Panther Party

title: Diary of a Bail-Out
description: A timestampped account of the step-by-step process of bailing out prisoners at Rikers Island, which processes prisoners from three different jails.

title: Free All Prostitutes, Free All Prisoners
description: This statement provides a list of demands by the Women's Bail Fund, such as an end to arrests for all "crimes" of survival, including prostitution, pre-trial detention, and more.

title: Free Our Sisters in the Rikers Concentration Camp
description: This flyer includes a statement in solidarity with the prison rebellion at Attica and the sympathy demonstration by women at Alderson prison. It lists the human rights abuses that take place at Rikers Island and charges Rockefeller for crimes committed against Black and brown populations, as well as the poor.

title: Free Our Sisters In Jail
description: This flyer includes information about the prison conditions impacting women held at Rikers Island, both without trial and serving sentences. It also describes the work of the Women's Bail Fund and calls for support through various ways of contributing.

title: Midnight Special: Prisoner News [WBF Articles]
description: Midnight Special is published by a coalition of Prisoner Rights groups in New York City with the aim of disseminating legal information to aid prisoner's rights. This issue includes an article about prison conditions at Rikers Island, an article on a rebellion at Alderson which involved a strike and demands for better pay, better medical care, and more. The issue also includes information and history about the work of the Women's Bail Fund.
publisher: National Lawyers Guild (NY Chapter) and The Prison Justice Committee

title: Press Release: Women's Bail Fund
description: A press release issued by the Women's Bail Fund in response to hearings related to prisoner suicides in New York City jails. The press release refers to these deaths as murders and the prison system a murderer.

title: Revolution is the Only Solution
description: This article comes from a series that was published in the Liberated Guardian. It includes the author's personal account of her brother, Johnny, a white working class person who struggled with drug addiction and sold drugs. Johnny was eventually convicted and paroled to Daytop Village, a drug treatment facility, as an alternative to a six-year jail sentence.

title: Women Prisoners [Flyer]
description: This flyer includes information drawn from an issue of Ramparts about the various facilities housing women prisoners, conditions inside women's prisons and jails, and a few statistics related to women's imprisonment.

title: Women's Bail Fund Letter
description: This letter requests support for the Women's Bail Fund and women detained at the Women's House of Detention. It indicates a new location of the Women's House of Detention at Riker's Island and describes conditions of isolation and forced labor at Riker's. It also provides some examples of prisoner's organizing and building power inside.

title: THE PLAGUE: Capitalism + Dope = GENOCIDE [Article]
description: This essay provides an iteration of a pamphlet with the same title, written by Michael Cetewayo Tabor while in prison. Michael was a political prisoner part of the NY Panther 21 case. The essay includes an introduction with some background information on the author. It was included as part of a packet of materials distributed by the Women's Bail Fund.

title: Demands of the Sisters Inside
description: A list of grievances and demands addressed to the concerned people of New York relating to medical care, prison food, pests and vermin, physical abuse, and more. It includes a poem by a Vietnamese woman prisoner and a quote by Ho Chi Minh.

title: This Has Been Sitting at the Back of My Brain...
description: This statement is by an unknown author who raises the importance of their time inside to their political development and reflects on their work with the Women's Bail Fund--challenges, successes, and lessons. Some of the topics discussed include selecting readings for political education, the approach to criticism self-criticism to address internal contradictions, taking leadership from third world women, combatting racism within the primarily white group, and more.

title: Capitalism Plus Dope Equals Genocide
description: This pamphlet, written by Michael "Cetewayo" Tabor, a political prisoner part of the Panther 21, deals with heroin and the issue of drug addiction, specifically its effects on the Black community of Harlem. This particular copy was reprinted by the Women's Bail Fund.

title: Angela is free! Now we must free Ruchell and all political prisoners!
description: Flyer with handwritten title announcing Angela Davis' release from prison while demanding the same for Ruchell Magee. Three typed paragraphs about Ruchell, Angela's codefendent, whose trial for murder-kidnap-consipiracy is to begin on August 1. Magee was the only person to survive the attempt to escape from the Marin County Courthouse in 1970, which was led by Jonathan Jackson. Magee is accused of killing Judge Harold Haley, but witnesses claim that police shot the judge while trying to stop the

title: A Jail is a Jail
description: Typed flyer on a sheet of blue paper, double-sided. Opens with a discussion of the June 1971 transfer of inmates from NYC's infamous Women's House of Detention to the newly constructed New York City Correctional Facility for Women, which was done suddenly to try to avoid protests. The new facility at Rikers Island is described as an "isolated penal colony" which the city of New York spent somewhere between 16 and 24 million dollars building. Corrections authorities called it a "unique hotel," wi

title: Pamphlet on The Women's Bail Fund
description: Folded pamphlet with a folder of a brick wall on the cover. Inside there's a quote from Eugene V. Debs on the lower class and prison. Contains informational content about The Women's Bail Fund and what it does. Headlines read: ""Sisters in the House of D Write to the Bail Fund," "Where Do We Get Our Funds?" "Women's Bail Fund," "who Do We Bail Out?" "The Women's House of Detention," "Bail is Ransom," "Who is Innocent Until Proven Guilty?" "The Real Criminals are Never Punished!" "Why are Women A

title: Women's Bail Fund: Platform and Program
description:
Two typed pages on yellow paper describing the mission and purpose of the Women's Bail Fund. It lists and elaborates on 7 key tenants of the organization, which are as follows:
"1. We want power to determine our destinies as women.
2. We demand an immediate release of all prisoners held in federal, state, county and city prisons and jails.
3. We demand an end to racism.
4. We demand an end to genocide perpetrated in the prisons. 5. We believe that armed self-defense groups and armed struggle are

title: Black Workers Council Organize to Fight!
The Black Liberation Struggle (Within the Current World Struggle)
description: A transcript which followed a talk by Bill Epton reflecting on the state of Black Liberation within the current context of the world and other struggles. This talk touched on a number of elements and topics, such as white racism, male chauvinism, Marxist- Leninist principles and the importance of the "Third World".

title: Electoral Politics: Its Problems & Prospects
description: Towards Clarity around Electoral Action; Ruling Class not always united; Apathy Among the People; Some partially successful campaigns in the past; the question of the slogan: don't vote, organize; the role of radicals in electoral struggle; parliamentarianism; a revolutionary electoral program; A brief summation of electoral action and the Philadelphia Black United Front.

title: Skatzes/Lucasville Five Support Bulletin No. 13
description: Bulletin representing George Skatzes and the Lucasville Five by mapping out the circumstances of the Rebellion and it's aftermath. This edition outlines some of the conditions at the Ohio State Penitentiary, the state supermax prison in Youngstown, OH where the Lucasville 5 were transferred. The bulletin contains information on issues of most concern to the prisoners at OSP including criteria for selection, lack of notice, mental health, hygiene, medical treatment, and grievance procedures.

The docudrama contains evidence not allowed in the trials of the Lucasville 5.
